summary: Lock CardsCarousel progress bar and navigation buttons to always use neon-green color, regardless of page accent color setting

currentImplementation.progressBar: The progress bar fill uses `background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--gradient-left) 0%, var(--gradient-right) 100%)` defined in resources/css/screen/swiper.css line 26. The --gradient-left and --gradient-right custom properties change per accent color class (.blue, .yellow, .orange, .purple, .green) in resources/css/screen/colors.css lines 209-237.
currentImplementation.navigation: Navigation buttons (.carousel-nav-btn) use the same gradient `linear-gradient(90deg, var(--gradient-left) 0%, var(--gradient-right) 100%)` via a ::before pseudo-element for the hover fill effect, defined in resources/css/screen/swiper.css line 50. On hover, the pseudo-element animates in with the carousel-nav-gradient-sweep keyframe animation.

neonGreenValues.notes: The default neon-green gradient goes from neon-green (--color-neon-green) to blue (--color-blue). These are the :root defaults for --gradient-left and --gradient-right. We need to hard-code these exact values so they do not change when .blue, .yellow, .orange, .purple, or .green classes are applied to ancestor elements.

fileChanges.description: Override --gradient-left and --gradient-right on .cards-carousel scope to lock them to neon-green values
fileChanges.details: Add a new rule block: `.cards-carousel { --gradient-left: rgba(212, 255, 69, 1); --gradient-right: rgba(71, 203, 242, 1); }` This scopes the gradient override to CardsCarousel only. The existing `.swiper-pagination-progressbar .swiper-pagination-progressbar-fill` and `.carousel-nav-btn::before` rules already reference --gradient-left/--gradient-right, so they will inherit the locked values when inside .cards-carousel. No changes needed to the gradient rules themselves.

designDecisions.decision: Scope the override to .cards-carousel class rather than hard-coding values in individual rules
designDecisions.rationale: The .carousel-nav-btn and .swiper-pagination-progressbar-fill styles are shared by multiple carousel blocks (e.g., TestimonialsCarousel also uses .carousel-nav-btn via the modal-close-btn pattern). By scoping to .cards-carousel, we only lock the color for CardsCarousel without affecting other blocks that may want to respect the page accent color.

designDecisions.decision: Use the neon-green-to-blue gradient (not solid neon-green)
designDecisions.rationale: The default gradient goes from neon-green (left) to blue (right). This is the brand default. The progress bar and nav button hover both use this two-color gradient. Locking to just neon-green would lose the gradient effect that is part of the design language.

designDecisions.decision: Use raw rgba values instead of var(--color-neon-green) / var(--color-blue)
designDecisions.rationale: Using CSS custom property references like var(--color-neon-green) would still work since those are defined in @theme and don't change per accent color. However, since --gradient-left/--gradient-right are overridden by accent color classes (.blue, .yellow, etc.) via specificity, we need our .cards-carousel override to win. Using either approach works since .cards-carousel is a more specific scope, but referencing the @theme tokens (var(--color-neon-green), var(--color-blue)) is actually cleaner and more maintainable. REVISED: Use var(--color-neon-green) and var(--color-blue) for maintainability.

implementation.approach: Add a single CSS rule scoped to .cards-carousel that resets --gradient-left and --gradient-right to the neon-green default values
implementation.insertionPoint: In resources/css/screen/swiper.css, after the progress bar styles (after line 29) or at the top of the file as a scoped override
implementation.alternativeApproach: Could also add a CardsCarousel.css file to the block, but since all carousel styles are centralized in swiper.css, keeping it there is more consistent

qaChecklist: Verify progress bar gradient stays neon-green-to-blue on a page with .blue accent color
Verify progress bar gradient stays neon-green-to-blue on a page with .orange accent color
Verify progress bar gradient stays neon-green-to-blue on a page with .purple accent color
Verify nav button hover fill stays neon-green-to-blue gradient on all accent color pages
Verify other carousel blocks (TestimonialsCarousel) still respect page accent color for their gradients
Verify dark mode still works correctly for CardsCarousel
Test in both editor (CardsCarousel.tsx) and frontend (CardsCarousel.js) views
